---
pagetitle: "Start with a Design-Loop Card - Architecture 2.0"
description: "Draft the context profile of an Architecture 2.0 design-loop card in 30 minutes, then complete only the review profiles supported by real records."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="site-wrap start-page">
<header class="site-masthead">
  <span class="pill">Architecture 2.0</span>
  <span class="pill hot">Context first</span>
</header>

<section class="site-hero start-hero">
  <div class="hub-kicker">Start with the core artifact</div>
  <h1>Draft one design-loop card</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">
    Name one decision, one typed claim, its comparator, and the boundary of the
    study. Complete inspectability, replay, independent-review, disclosure, and
    decision-right profiles only when their records exist.
  </p>
  <nav class="site-actions" aria-label="Design-loop card starting points">
    <a class="site-action" href="/design-loop-card/template.yaml" download>Download schema 2.0 YAML <span class="arr" aria-hidden="true">&darr;</span></a>
    <a class="site-action secondary" href="/examples/design-loop-cards/array-study-v2.yaml">Inspect the v2 example</a>
    <a class="site-action tertiary" href="/book/appendices/appendix-b-design-loop-card/">Read Appendix B</a>
  </nav>
</section>

<section class="start-workflow" aria-labelledby="draft-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="draft-title">Draft the context profile in 30 minutes</h2>
    <p>The context profile is useful before a study runs. It does not claim that evidence exists, replay works, review is independent, or anyone has authorized execution.</p>
  </div>

  <ol class="start-step-list">
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">01</span>
        <div><p>5 minutes</p><h3>Choose one decision</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Write one bounded architecture decision or experiment. Avoid goals such as &ldquo;improve the system&rdquo; that do not identify what could advance next.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">02</span>
        <div><p>8 minutes</p><h3>State the claim</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Name the claim type, statement, baseline or comparator, expected or observed outcome, scope, non-claims, status, and evidence links. A proposed or untested claim may have no evidence links; a supported claim may not.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">03</span>
        <div><p>7 minutes</p><h3>Draw the design-space boundary</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>List legal choices, invalid choices, and work deferred to a later turn. A reviewer should be able to tell where this study must stop.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">04</span>
        <div><p>5 minutes</p><h3>Name who decides</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Record the pending decision, who owns it, the action under consideration, the action currently authorized, and the evidence that would require another review. A pending decision is valid; it does not mean the card is unfinished.</p>
    </li>
    <li class="start-step">
      <div class="start-step-heading">
        <span class="start-step-number" aria-hidden="true">05</span>
        <div><p>5 minutes</p><h3>Validate the structure</h3></div>
      </div>
      <p>Run the validator from a repository clone and fix every reported path.</p>
      <pre class="start-command"><code>.venv-card/bin/python cli/arch2.py validate card my-card.yaml</code></pre>
    </li>
  </ol>
</section>

<aside class="site-note" aria-labelledby="validation-limit-title">
  <span class="site-note-label">Interpretation limit</span>
  <p id="validation-limit-title"><strong>Schema validation proves structure and local bindings, not evidence adequacy.</strong> For schema 2.0, the validator also checks internal references, declared decision rights, and hashes of local artifacts. It does not prove that the workload is representative, the chosen fidelity supports the commitment, an external artifact is unchanged, or an independent reviewer is actually independent.</p>
</aside>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="profiles-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="profiles-title">Complete profiles independently</h2>
    <p>Profiles are not maturity levels. Mark each one <code>complete</code>, <code>partial</code>, or <code>not_claimed</code>. A partial profile must name its gaps.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <div class="artifact-link"><span class="site-pill">Always</span><strong>Context</strong><span>Intent, open task description, design-space boundaries, typed claims, and a pending or recorded decision</span></div>
    <div class="artifact-link"><span class="site-pill">Inspect</span><strong>Inspectability</strong><span>Representation, environment, method roles, feedback budget, typed evidence, failed or rejected records, and predeclared rejection checks</span></div>
    <div class="artifact-link"><span class="site-pill">Execute</span><strong>Replay</strong><span>Commands, environment binding, hashed inputs and outputs, expected and observed status, and verified validation result</span></div>
    <div class="artifact-link"><span class="site-pill">Challenge</span><strong>Independent review</strong><span>Reviewer identity, independence basis, shared dependencies, conflicts, result, and claim and evidence links</span></div>
    <div class="artifact-link"><span class="site-pill">Release</span><strong>Disclosure</strong><span>Data classes, redactions, reviewer roles, and the release or compliance boundary</span></div>
    <div class="artifact-link"><span class="site-pill hot">Authority</span><strong>Decision-right completeness</strong><span>Who may propose, execute, reject, waive, recommend, and commit, including scope and conditions</span></div>
  </div>
  <p class="site-lede">A card can complete independent review without public disclosure, or disclosure without replay. One profile never implies another.</p>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="example-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="example-title">Inspect one complete synthetic example</h2>
    <p>The v2 example binds a deterministic estimator, a synthetic workload, two evidence packets, typed rejection checks, replay, review, and decision rights. Its evidence status is <code>illustrative</code>, so the card makes no empirical architecture claim.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/examples/design-loop-cards/array-study-v2.yaml"><span class="site-pill hot">Schema 2.0</span><strong>Read the complete card</strong><span>Trace each claim to evidence, rejection checks, replay, and an authorized action</span></a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/tree/main/examples/design-loop-cards"><span class="site-pill">Replay</span><strong>Verify the packets</strong><span>Run the recorded command and inspect the hash-bound inputs and outputs</span></a>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="labs-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="labs-title">Practice with executed evidence</h2>
    <p>The synthetic fixture isolates the contract. It does not replace empirical lab work with tool-produced evidence.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2/tree/main/labs">
      <span class="site-pill hot">Simulator-backed</span>
      <strong>Run the Architecture 2.0 labs</strong>
      <span>Generate SCALE-Sim evidence, inspect raw outputs, preserve failures and rejections, and record a decision with a named owner</span>
    </a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/book/chapters/08-running-the-loop/">
      <span class="site-pill">Executed study</span>
      <strong>Study the Chapter 8 case</strong>
      <span>Follow an AI-assisted, equal-budget study from preregistration through a null result and stop decision</span>
    </a>
  </div>
  <p class="site-lede">The synthetic fixture, simulator-backed exercise, and Chapter 8 study serve different teaching roles. None substitutes for another.</p>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="axes-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="axes-title">Keep profiles, evidence fidelity, and commitment separate</h2>
    <p>A complete profile says that named bindings exist. It does not raise evidence fidelity or authorize a larger action.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <div class="artifact-link"><span class="site-pill">Contract</span><strong>Profile completeness</strong><span>Which review bindings are structurally present and internally consistent</span></div>
    <div class="artifact-link"><span class="site-pill">Measurement</span><strong>Evidence fidelity</strong><span>How closely the workload, tool, and observations represent the target system</span></div>
    <div class="artifact-link"><span class="site-pill">Decision</span><strong>Commitment</strong><span>What action the named decision owner authorizes and how reversible it is</span></div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="setup-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="setup-title">Set up the validator once</h2>
    <p>The isolated environment installs only the card validator. It does not install the book, PDF, plotting, or notebook toolchain.</p>
  </div>
  <pre class="start-command"><code>git clone https://github.com/harvard-edge/arch2.git
cd arch2
python3 -m venv .venv-card
.venv-card/bin/python -m pip install --upgrade pip==26.1.2
.venv-card/bin/python -m pip install -r requirements-card.txt
.venv-card/bin/python cli/arch2.py validate card my-card.yaml</code></pre>
  <p class="start-step-note">The validator rejects unsupported versions, unknown fields, incomplete typed claims or evidence, false profile claims, broken internal references, incomplete decision rights, and mismatched local hashes.</p>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="legacy-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="legacy-title">Migrate v1.1 without inventing evidence</h2>
    <p>Schema 1.1 remains readable for one public release. Its cumulative Level 0–3 files are legacy fixtures, not the model for new work.</p>
  </div>
  <pre class="start-command"><code>./arch2 migrate card old-card.yaml --output old-card.v2-migration-draft.yaml</code></pre>
  <p class="site-lede">The converter maps only fields with a direct meaning and lists unresolved semantics. The output remains a migration draft until an author supplies claim status, evidence status, typed rejection checks, replay validation, and decision rights.</p>
</section>

<section class="site-section" aria-labelledby="artifacts-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="artifacts-title">Working artifacts</h2>
    <p>The schema defines the contract. The rubric and evidence review determine whether a claim deserves trust.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="artifact-link-grid">
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/schemas/design-loop-card.v2.schema.json"><span class="site-pill">Contract</span><strong>JSON Schema v2.0</strong><span>Machine-readable claims, evidence, rejection checks, profiles, replay, and decision rights</span></a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://arch2.mlsysbook.ai/design-loop-card/template.md" download><span class="site-pill">Review</span><strong>One-page Markdown form</strong><span>A human-readable card for paper and project review</span></a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="https://arch2.mlsysbook.ai/schemas/design-loop-card.md"><span class="site-pill">Compatibility</span><strong>Version policy</strong><span>Extension, migration, compatibility, and change rules</span></a>
    <a class="artifact-link" href="/examples/design-loop-cards/array-study-v2.yaml"><span class="site-pill">Example</span><strong>Canonical v2 card</strong><span>A complete synthetic record with executable replay</span></a>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="site-section instructor-exercise" aria-labelledby="exercise-title">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2 id="exercise-title">Instructor exercise</h2>
    <p>Pairs can complete a context-profile paper-to-loop draft in one 30-minute block.</p>
  </div>
  <ol>
    <li><strong>Choose, 5 minutes.</strong> Select one paper with a concrete architecture result and name the next decision its result could support.</li>
    <li><strong>Claim, 8 minutes.</strong> Write the statement, comparator, outcome, scope, non-claims, status, and available evidence links.</li>
    <li><strong>Bound, 7 minutes.</strong> List legal, invalid, and deferred design choices.</li>
    <li><strong>Assign, 5 minutes.</strong> Name the pending decision holder and current authorization boundary.</li>
    <li><strong>Challenge, 5 minutes.</strong> Exchange cards and identify one hidden assumption, unsupported status, or ambiguous boundary.</li>
  </ol>
  <p class="start-step-note">For a follow-on exercise, give students an existing evidence packet and ask them to complete the inspectability profile. Do not ask them to fabricate evidence, failures, or independent review that a paper does not report.</p>
</section>
</div>
```
